expert_rss.summary:
On Saturday, for the second time in a week, U.S. negotiators were set to travel to Islamabad to continue talks with their Iranian counterparts, only to have their departure canceled as Tehran pulled back or set new conditions for the meeting. The discussions were to take place at a lower level of representation than the […]

expert_rss.summary:
April 26, 2026, marked the fortieth anniversary of the Chornobyl nuclear disaster. expert_rss.title: Ashlyn Kalaher Named Chief Strategy Officer at Sentinel
expert_rss.summary: Sentinel has appointed Ashlyn Kalaher as its new Chief Strategy Officer, bringing a background that spans FBI federal intelligence and private-sector security leadership.

expert_rss.title: Leslie McLane Joins Virginia Public Safety and Homeland Security Secretariat in Communications Role
expert_rss.summary: Leslie McLane has joined the Virginia Public Safety and Homeland Security Secretariat as Director of Communications and Strategic Projects.

expert_rss.title: Air Force Releases Data and AI Strategies to Guide Future Operations
expert_rss.summary: The Department of the Air Force’s Office of the Chief Data and Artificial Intelligence Officer has announced the public release of its DAF Data and AI Strategies.

expert_rss.title: NATO Podcast Examines Iran Conflict and Implications for Global Security
expert_rss.summary: A new episode of NATO’s DEEP Dive podcast is taking a closer look at how the ongoing Iran conflict is shaping the security landscape across the Middle East and beyond.

expert_rss.title: DHS S&T Technologically Speaking Podcast Opens New Season With Focus on Chemical Threats
expert_rss.summary: The DHS Science and Technology Directorate has launched Season 6 of its Technologically Speaking podcast with a discussion on the science behind chemical threat preparedness and response.

expert_rss.title: Disaster & After Podcast Explores Leadership and Loss in the Wake of Columbine High School Shooting
expert_rss.summary: The latest episode of Disaster & After turns to one of the most difficult questions in crisis response: what comes after the immediate aftermath, when there are no clear answers and no words that can ease the loss.

expert_rss.title: Preventing Targeted Violence in a Decentralized Threat Environment
expert_rss.summary: Recent incidents of attempted or near-targeted violence against public officials and at high-visibility events have heightened concern about the trajectory of domestic threat activity in the United States.

expert_rss.title: 15 Chemical Spraying Drones Stolen in New Jersey as FBI Investigates Possible Weaponization Scenario
expert_rss.summary: Someone stole 15 giant agricultural drones in New Jersey — and experts fear a “nightmare scenario” if they end up in the hands of terrorists.

expert_rss.title: Brian Cavanaugh Expected to be Tapped as Under Secretary of Management at DHS
expert_rss.summary: President Trump is expected to tap former senior Office of Management and Budget official Brian Cavanaugh to serve as Under Secretary of Management at the Department of Homeland Security, according to two sources familiar with the matter.

expert_rss.title: Seebald & Associates International and The SRI Group Form Strategic Alliance to Bolster Port Security Against Drone Threats
expert_rss.summary: Seebald and Associates has announced the establishment of a strategic partnership with The SRI Group LLC, a provider of detailed Counter Unmanned Aircraft Systems (C/UAS) threat assessments.

expert_rss.title: Navy Commissions Submarine USS Idaho
expert_rss.summary: The Navy commissioned the submarine USS Idaho in a traditional ceremony at Naval Submarine Base New London in Groton, Conn., bringing cutting-edge warfighting capability to the nation's undersea forces after a multiyear construction process.

expert_rss.title: U.S., Moroccan Air Forces Project Combat Power During Exercise African Lion 26
expert_rss.summary: During Exercise African Lion 26, a joint flyover of U.S. and Moroccan aircraft inaugurated Morocco's first accredited joint terminal attack controller qualification course to enhance regional interoperability.

expert_rss.title: MP7 Personal Defense Weapon Just Went Viral In Hands Of Tailored-Suit Wearing Agent
expert_rss.summary:
The cool under pressure agent yanked the MP7 from a discreet backpack after a shooter tried to storm the White House Correspondents' Dinner.
The post MP7 Personal Defense Weapon Just Went Viral In Hands Of Tailored-Suit Wearing Agent appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Russia’s Stealthy S-71K Air-Launched Missile Seen In New Detail
expert_rss.summary:
The missile aims to be a less expensive, but still survivable standoff weapon option that can be built in large numbers.
The post Russia’s Stealthy S-71K Air-Launched Missile Seen In New Detail appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Prospects Dimming On Iran-U.S. Deal To Open Strait, End War
expert_rss.summary:
Tehran has delivered a new offer to Washington that is unlikely to move the needle as blockade aims to cripple Iran's oil infrastructure.
The post Prospects Dimming On Iran-U.S. Deal To Open Strait, End War appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Evidence Of Ukraine Using AIM-120C-8 Missiles Emerges
expert_rss.summary:
Wreckage imagery indicates Ukraine is fielding near-top-tier AIM-120C-8s, which are employable via F-16s and NASAMS launchers.
The post Evidence Of Ukraine Using AIM-120C-8 Missiles Emerges appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Rare Video Appears To Show A Soviet Su-7 Jet Dropping A Nuclear Bomb
expert_rss.summary:
During one very unusual Cold War drill, a Soviet Su-7 Fitter attack jet delivered a live tactical nuclear weapon in an end-to-end test.
The post Rare Video Appears To Show A Soviet Su-7 Jet Dropping A Nuclear Bomb appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Carrier Tracker As of April 26, 2026
expert_rss.summary:
The U.S. now has three carrier strike groups in the U.S. Central Command area of responsibility for the first time in more than two decades.
The post Carrier Tracker As of April 26, 2026 appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Why aircraft carriers are the best (and worst) place for laser weapons
expert_rss.summary: High-energy laser weapons are a natural fit for large, power-rich aircraft carriers — with limits.

expert_rss.title: What we know about the US military’s new joint laser weapon system
expert_rss.summary: The Army-Navy effort aims to produce a containerized 150-kilowatt high-energy laser weapon to counter incoming cruise missiles.

expert_rss.title: A missed lane and a comeback: Ranger team wins Best Sapper
expert_rss.summary: On the first day of the Army’s most elite combat engineering competition, the winning team made an error that could have knocked them out of running.

expert_rss.title: US Air Force seeks $3.9 billion to boost KC-46 fleet
expert_rss.summary: The service also clarified plans for the size of its KC-46 fleet.

expert_rss.title: All Marines required to follow grooming standards within 12 months or face separation
expert_rss.summary: The Corps announced that even those who request exemptions for medical conditions can face administrative separation after 12 months of non-compliance.

expert_rss.title: Vietnam veteran, daughter sue VA over Agent Orange birth defect benefits
expert_rss.summary: The VA provides disability compensation for birth defects to the children of women Vietnam veterans, but not to the children of men who served in the war.
